summary: A stellar audience capture of a June 1977 Winterland show, highlighted by an epic seven‑minute “The Music Never Stopped” and a transcendent “Morning Dew,” with crystal‑clear tone and flawless playing.
review: The recording is praised as a dynamic, high‑fidelity audience tape that lets every instrument shine—especially Jerry’s newly restored Wolf guitar. Musically the concert is described as a high‑water mark for 1977, with the first‑set closer “The Music Never Stopped” and the soaring “Morning Dew” standing out above an otherwise superb setlist.

ai_rating.rationale: All ten reviews award five stars, repeatedly call the show a masterpiece and the recording “dynamic” or “crisp.” Both performance and audio quality are consistently praised, leaving virtually no doubt of an exceptional rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Reviewers describe the tape as dynamic, crystal‑clear and well balanced—no dropouts or muffiness noted. The audience capture is said to rival soundboard quality.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows smoothly from upbeat first‑set numbers into a soaring second set; the seven‑minute “The Music Never Stopped” closes the first set spectacularly, while “Morning Dew” crowns the night as the emotional high point.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone is repeatedly lauded—his Wolf guitar is described as ‘flaming,’ with hurricane‑like overtones; reviewers note this may be the definitive capture of that instrument.
band_member_comments.Phil: Bass is noted as less prominent but more accurate than many SBD recordings, and its sound is praised for clarity.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s rhythm work and vocal harmonies are highlighted as part of the “beautiful chords” that flood the Dew segment.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s keyboard textures receive praise, especially his use of Mutron effects on “Estimated Prophet” and contributions to “Morning Dew.”
